title: teramnus labialis subsp. labialis
intro: Teramnus labialis subsp. labialis, also known as the Black Eyed Pea or Horse Eyeball, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, belonging to the legume family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which are attributed to its high content of alkaloids, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds. The plant is said to offer various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and treating wounds and skin conditions, due to its antiseptic and antispasmodic properties. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a herbal remedy through infusions, decoctions, and poultices, which are used to treat various ailments, demonstrating its potential as a valuable medicinal plant in traditional medicine.
